title: TIME DEFICIT REALITY CHECK

heading_structure.title: WHAT'S LEFT FOR WAGE WORK: 4–11 hours/day

code_blocks:
import json
from src.score import aggregate

# General sensor evaluation pattern
sensor = {
    "id": "general_assessment",
    "signals": [
        {"name": "primary_signal", "weight": 0.6, "_value": 0.5},
        {"name": "secondary_signal", "weight": 0.4, "_value": 0.5}
    ],
    "scoring": {"aggregation": "weighted_mean"},
    "thresholds": {"concern": 0.20, "notice": 0.40, "healthy": 0.70}
}

score = aggregate(sensor["scoring"]["aggregation"], sensor["signals"])
thresholds = sensor["thresholds"]
band = ("concern" if score <= thresholds["concern"] else
        "notice" if score <= thresholds["notice"] else
        "healthy" if score >= thresholds["healthy"] else "neutral")
print(f"Score: {score:.3f}, Band: {band}")
lists: Sleep: 7–8 hours (biological)
Eating/food prep: 2–3 hours (thermodynamic)
Childcare: 2–4 hours (social reproduction)
Commute: 1–3 hours (geographic)
System interface: 1–2 hours (institutional)
BEFORE economic analysis: Calculate time feasibility
TREAT time as primary constraint, money as secondary
VALIDATE all assumptions against 24-hour reality
RECOGNIZE exit as rational adaptation, not failure
Income insufficient → “financial stress” → reliance on government programs
Workforce participation interpreted as constrained primarily by money
Parents drop second job or reduce work hours → net time regained
Cook at home, garden, barter → direct survival resource acquisition
Children stay at home longer → shared labor
Household becomes self-supporting node, not market-dependent
Reduced formal labor participation seems like “economic withdrawal”
But hidden labor (community, family, self-production) maintains survival efficiency
Time-deficit reduction is the rational adaptive signal
Traditional study sees financial stress → adaptive behavior misclassified as failure
Time-centric view sees behavior as rational optimization of survival
Money is proxy variable; ignoring time cost of survival tasks introduces systematic misinterpretation
Coherent institutional framing
Metric improvement
Stability preservation
Downstream consequence analysis
Humans operating outside institutions
Physical resource depletion
Thermodynamic coupling across domains
Accumulating stress thresholds
Simultaneous collapse scenarios
Ask: "Who is missing from my data?"
Check: "What physical resources are assumed?"
Couple: "Are these domains actually separate?"
Validate: "Does this match ground truth observation?"
Listen: "Where are humans exiting and why?"
